perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time while the Employee is living by written notice thereof by either the Employer or the Employee to the other; and, in any event, this Agreement will terminate upon termination of the Employee's employment.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time while the Employee is living by written notice thereof by the Employer with 30 days notice, but the Employee can not terminate the agreement; and, in any event, this Agreement will terminate upon termination of the Employee's employment.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that only the Employer can terminate the agreement with a 30-day notice and that Employee can not terminate the agreement, contradicting the initial statement that either party could terminate. This creates uncertainty and a contradiction regarding the termination rights of the Employee and employer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time while the Employee is living by written notice thereof by the Employer with 30 days notice, but the Employee can not terminate the agreement; and, in any event, this Agreement will terminate upon termination of the Employee's employment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'the Employee can not terminate the agreement,' which directly contradicts the original text's provision allowing termination 'by either the Employer or the Employee.' This creates a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency regarding the Employee's termination rights, violating the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of a contract providing conflicting obligations.
